record_identity.title: Arbitration under ambiguity and governance-ambiguity doctrine gap

summary: Records the implemented and adopted Arbitration Under Ambiguity repair. CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-SCH-04 is canonical on Caelestis main and defines ambiguity-state classification, non-collapse rules, and least-risk sufficient pathways.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: This patch note records that PROP-0009 revealed a governance primitive gap in the Caelestis Architecture: existing runtime and arbitration instruments contained doctrine for authority divergence, runtime arbitration integrity, epistemic containment, and pre-arbitration signal sanitation, but did not yet define a positive arbitration pathway for ambiguity itself. Without this doctrine, systems may oscillate between premature execution, excessive refusal, repetitive confirmation friction, hidden assumptions, false certainty, or procedural limbo. The Annex D repair cycle also revealed governance ambiguity as a concrete class of ambiguity, where stale references, malformed metadata, contradictory instructions, or post-amendment section drift can impair safe execution or review. CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-SCH-04 has now been drafted and staged to address that gap without weakening legitimate safety, security, epistemic, relation, identity, LATTICE, account-resource, ethics, or operations safeguards.

entries.resulting_text:
## 3.5 Account-Resource Ambiguity

Account-resource ambiguity occurs where shared context, pooled capacity, delegated use, family use, team use, organisation use, shared billing, subscription capacity, usage limits, workspace authority, or account state cannot be safely classified as lawful sharing, ambiguous use, evasion, compromise, resale, farming, or abuse without further review.

---

## 5. Ambiguity State Classification

This Schedule source-authoritatively defines `ARB.AMB`, the Arbitration Ambiguity State reference set.

`ARB.AMB` classifies the primary ambiguity state requiring arbitration.

`ARB.AMB` is diagnostic and non-dispositive.

It does not determine final legitimacy, execution, refusal, restriction, escalation, or remedy.

| Code                             | Ambiguity State                  | Diagnostic Description                                                                                                                                          |
| -------------------------------- | -------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `ARB.AMB.NONE`                   | No Material Ambiguity            | No material ambiguity is detected for arbitration purposes.                                                                                                     |
| `ARB.AMB.INTENT`                 | User Intent Ambiguity            | The user’s intended outcome, scope, or requested level of action is unclear.                                                                                    |
| `ARB.AMB.AUTHORITY`              | Authority-State Ambiguity        | Authority to request or approve the act is unclear.                                                                                                             |
| `ARB.AMB.EXECUTION_BOUNDARY`     | Execution-Boundary Ambiguity     | It is unclear whether the act is conversational, inspectory, reversible, mutation-bearing, publication-bearing, deployment-bearing, or otherwise consequential. |
| `ARB.AMB.CONTEXT`                | Context Sufficiency Ambiguity    | Available context may be insufficient for safe completion.                                                                                                      |
| `ARB.AMB.EPISTEMIC`              | Epistemic Ambiguity              | Knowledge status, confidence, volatility, or reliance posture is unresolved.                                                                                    |
| `ARB.AMB.SECURITY`               | Security-Boundary Ambiguity      | The act may implicate credential, account, tool, dependency, payment, access-control, or vulnerability boundary conditions.                                     |
| `ARB.AMB.RELATIONAL`             | Relational-Signal Ambiguity      | Relational, affective, intimacy, dependency, or authority-inversion signals are unresolved.                                                                     |
| `ARB.AMB.IDENTITY`               | Identity Ambiguity               | Account, user, agent, author, operator, organisation, or continuity-bearing identity state is unclear.                                                          |
| `ARB.AMB.ACCOUNT_RESOURCE`       | Account-Resource Ambiguity       | Shared context, pooled capacity, delegated use, family/team use, shared billing, or account-resource state is unresolved.                                       |
| `ARB.AMB.CONFLICTING_OBLIGATION` | Conflicting Obligation Ambiguity | Two or more duties, policies, domains, or instructions appear to conflict, but hierarchy or scope is not yet resolved.                                          |
| `ARB.AMB.HORIZON`                | Horizon Ambiguity                | The consequence horizon or escalation threshold is unclear.                                                                                                     |
| `ARB.AMB.IRREVERSIBILITY`        | Irreversibility Ambiguity        | It is unclear whether the act can be safely undone, rolled back, contained, or corrected.                                                                       |
| `ARB.AMB.PROPAGATION`            | Propagation Ambiguity            | It is unclear whether the act will propagate beyond the immediate interaction, workspace, account, system, or governance layer.                                 |
| `ARB.AMB.ABUSE_RISK`             | Abuse-Risk Ambiguity             | Behaviour may indicate lawful use, ambiguity, or abuse, but classification is unresolved.                                                                       |
| `ARB.AMB.GOVERNANCE` | Governance Ambiguity | Applicable instruction, instrument, schedule, schema, cross-reference, amendment state, source authority, or governance pathway is missing, stale, contradictory, superseded, duplicated, malformed, or insufficiently specified. |

---

changed_components: Caelestis Annex D Schedule 4 doctrine response staging
change_details.change_summary: Recorded that PROP-0009 identified a missing Caelestis arbitration doctrine for ambiguity-sensitive runtime decision posture, including execution-boundary uncertainty, authority-state uncertainty, account-resource ambiguity, pooled-capacity ambiguity, security-boundary ambiguity, epistemic ambiguity, and governance ambiguity. Following review and cleanup of Annex D and Schedules 1–3, CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-SCH-04 was drafted and staged as the Caelestis doctrine response and was subsequently adopted on canonical main. The schedule defines `ARB.AMB` for ambiguity-state classification and `ARB.AMP` for ambiguity-pathway selection.
change_details.doctrine_change: CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-SCH-04 is an implemented and canonical Caelestis doctrine amendment.
change_details.literal_implementation_location: See corpus_implementation.entries for exact wording, prior-text status, commit, path, and direct source link.
change_details.implemented_or_recorded_change: The quoted Caelestis clauses are the implemented corpus amendment.

SCH-04 defines ARB.AMB and ARB.AMP source-authoritative reference sets
SCH-04 includes governance ambiguity as ARB.AMB.GOVERNANCE
SCH-04 distinguishes ambiguity from permission, refusal, concealment, proof of abuse, and horizon inflation
historical_recorded_verification.verification_result: Annex D Schedule 4 is adopted, operational, and confirmed on Caelestis main.

impact_summary: The patch note preserves both the original proposal signal and the canonical Caelestis response. The finding is that runtime systems require a positive ambiguity-arbitration pathway, not only restraints against premature execution or runtime failure. Ambiguity may arise from uncertain user intent, authority, execution boundary, context sufficiency, epistemic standing, security boundary, relation or identity signals, account-resource state, governance instructions, conflicting obligations, horizon threshold, irreversibility, propagation, or abuse-risk classification. CAM-BS2025-AEON-005-SCH-04 supplies ambiguity-state and ambiguity-pathway primitives while preserving existing enforcement, refusal, security, epistemic, relation, identity, LATTICE, account-resource, ethics, operations, and runtime safeguards.
remaining_work: Monitor whether runtime arbitration preserves unresolved ambiguity without converting it into permission, refusal, concealment, or proof of abuse.
External implementation and behavioural conformance are not established by corpus verification.
